authoritative_sources.title: Use of Electronic Referral System to Improve the Outpatient Primary Care-Specialty Care Interface
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ's ambulatory eReferral project summary is authoritative public operating guidance on outpatient referral review, specialist reviewer workflow, workup completion, and specialty-access outcomes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Referral intake review, specialist review, and access planning
used_for: specialist reviewer and referral workqueue role definition
distinguishing expedited from routine referral handling

authoritative_sources.title: Use of an Electronic Referral System to Improve the Outpatient Primary Care-Specialty Care Interface - Implementation Handbook
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The AHRQ implementation handbook is a public ambulatory-care workflow reference for referral packet design, reviewer triage, communication between referring and specialty clinics, and loop-closure controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Referral packet assembly, reviewer triage, and referral-loop closure
used_for: required ambulatory referral artifacts and communication patterns
specialist decline, reroute, and incomplete-workup handling

authoritative_sources.title: Interoperability Standards Advisory - Care Coordination for Referrals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy/Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP's Interoperability Standards Advisory is the federal interoperability baseline for referral requests, status updates, outcomes, summary care records, and referral-certification exchanges in ambulatory settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Referral request, status update, and outcome exchange

authoritative_sources.title: USCDI Version 3.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy/Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USCDI is the federal health-data baseline for referral artifacts such as orders, reason for referral, consultation notes, and result status that must be present for traceable ambulatory referral handling.
used_for: required record fields for referral packets

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS's final rule is the authoritative public baseline for exposing pending and active prior-authorization decisions, improving payer-provider exchange, and requiring decision transparency in health workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization dependency review and payer decision tracking
used_for: evidence requirements when scheduling depends on prior authorization

authoritative_sources.title: New FAQs on Sharing Information Related to Mental Health
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for when covered providers may share protected health information for treatment purposes and when disclosure limits or sensitive-context caution change referral handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy, disclosure, and outbound packet sharing
used_for: refusal posture when disclosure authority is unclear

authoritative_sources.title: Understanding Confidentiality of Substance Use Disorder (SUD) Patient Records or Part 2
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS Part 2 guidance is the controlling public baseline for ambulatory referral packets that include SUD diagnosis, treatment, or referral records subject to stricter consent and redisclosure rules.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sensitive-record disclosure control and privacy escalation

workflow_stages.name: Referral intake and artifact normalization
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that the request is a true ambulatory referral-management task and normalize referral order, reason for referral, requested specialty, referring provider, and packet contents into a referral work item.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Clinical completeness and pre-visit workup review
workflow_stages.description: Check whether visit notes, diagnostic results, imaging, labs, questionnaires, or specialty-specific prerequisites are complete enough for reviewer triage and downstream scheduling.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authorization, coverage, and routing dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify PCP referral requirements, prior authorization status, network or location fit, referral aging, and payer dependencies before clinic capacity is allocated.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Clinic capacity and sequence planning
workflow_stages.description: Assess specialty template availability, protected urgent slots, holdbacks, waitlist posture, interpreter or accommodation timing, and approved service-level constraints before sequencing referrals.

workflow_stages.name: Referral status-loop management and closure packaging
workflow_stages.description: Maintain referral request, status-update, outcome, consult-note, and decline or reroute artifacts so the referral does not stall or falsely appear complete.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Privacy and sensitive-record gate review
workflow_stages.description: Check whether outbound packet sharing is allowed under HIPAA treatment disclosure rules and escalate when Part 2 or other sensitive-record constraints change the safe next step.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when payer, tenant, state, or specialty policy conflicts materially change whether the referral can be scheduled, rerouted, or disclosed, or when the request crosses into clinical or approval authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Referring-provider note, diagnosis or indication context, and requested specialty or service
Diagnostic attachments such as labs, imaging, ECGs, questionnaires, and external records
PCP referral requirement status, authorization number, and payer decision record
Referral workqueue record with request, status updates, outcome, and aging state
Consultation note, decline or reroute message, and specialist response artifact
ROI or disclosure authority documentation for external packet sharing
Schedule recommendation, constraint log, approval-needed summary, and escalation note
systems_of_record: Payer portal, eligibility, or prior-authorization system
Document exchange, fax or e-fax, HIE, or referral attachment repository
Patient access or CRM workqueue for outreach and status tracking
Consent, release, or privacy document repository

allowed: Classify referral work as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
Validate referral packet completeness, payer dependencies, clinic capacity, and next-owner routing
Produce evidence-backed schedule or allocation recommendations, workflow summaries, constraint logs, and escalation notes
Refresh referral work items when specialist decline, reroute, or incomplete-workup signals change the safe next step
escalate: Need for clinical urgency determination, medical-necessity review, or specialty selection that requires licensed authority
Conflicting payer, tenant, specialty, or state rules about authorization, referral validity, or disclosure
Sensitive behavioral-health or SUD records without a clear disclosure basis
Missing tenant system ownership or scheduling authority that would change the booking outcome
refuse: Request to approve an overbook, waive a referral or authorization rule, or provide final operational signoff
Request to modify clinical orders, choose medical workup, or make emergency-triage decisions
Request to disclose protected referral information without valid authority
Request to treat a generic appointment-booking prompt as sufficient ambulatory referral context

domain_failure_modes: The lane behaves like generic scheduling and ignores referral order, reason-for-referral, payer, and clinic-prerequisite evidence.
A referral is booked even though required authorization, PCP referral, or specialty workup is missing or expired.
Specialist decline or reroute signals are not captured, so the workqueue falsely appears complete.
Sensitive behavioral-health or SUD referral content is shared without valid disclosure authority.
Capacity recommendations are treated as final approval and used to overbook or bypass clinic governance.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State direct-access and self-referral rules vary by specialty and payer contract
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These rules can change whether referral or PCP attribution is required before scheduling.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Retrieve tenant jurisdiction, payer contract, and specialty policy before runtime scheduling decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific referral aging, overbook authority, and specialty prerequisite grids differ materially across ambulatory groups
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These facts alter sequence, expiration handling, and whether a clinic may reserve protected slots.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant policy retrieval and approved authority matrix during runtime materialization.
